title: Rental Deposit Assistance * Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Rental Deposit Assistance * Veterans 19 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Agricultural Laborers AIDS/HIV Families with Children Foster Children Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues LGBTQ2+ Individuals Medicaid Recipients Native American Community * Homelessness Issues Older Adults Pregnant Individuals Slavic Community Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ACCESS SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program with assistance through case management. Support may include move-in costs, rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, and legal aid around housing barriers. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. May require clients to be alcohol-and drug-free. Assistance depends on the availability of funding and eligibility. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: -Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness -Veteran must be present in household -Any discharge other than dishonorable Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (458) 488-1340 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLUE MOUNTAIN ACTION COUNCIL SSVF SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless within 30 days or less. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 312 N 2nd Avenue Walla Walla, WA 99362 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-4pm Email (509) 529-4980 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Eligible veterans and their families can be supported in obtaining housing or avoiding eviction in their current housing. Case management support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention (rental and utility arrears), benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. All temporary financial assistance is subject to the Veteran's eligibility according to SSVF/VA program policies. Financial assistance depends on availability of funds. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household and if housed on the lease. Members of a household are determined by the Veteran. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for veterans and their families.Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. May require clients to be alcohol- and drug-free. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness and have an income at or below 50% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term housing assistance. Support may include help with finding housing, move-in or rental assistance, eviction prevention, and access to benefits, employment, education, legal services related to housing barriers, and other expenses that support stable housing. Case management is available, pending eligibility. Participation requires working with a caseworker, and some programs may require individuals to be drug- and alcohol-free. All assistance is based on eligibility and the availability of funding. 125 N 17th Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than Dishonorable or General Court Martial. Veteran must be present in the household. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Email (503) 397-3511 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY BEAVERTON MULTI-SERVICE CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Hours: Appointment hours: Monday-Wednesday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Walk in hours: Thursday 9am-noon, first come, first served Email (541) 573-1342 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL THE GLORIA CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program. Veteran must be present in the household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (541) 298-5131 200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers housing-focused case management, referrals to community services, limited short-term rental or deposit assistance, and housing placement support. All assistance is time-limited and based on program guidelines. 1855 Thomas Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Veteran households may be considered for SSVF if they: -Include at least one eligible veteran (per VA guidelines) -Are homeless or at risk of homelessness -Have a household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) for Coos County -Meet additional SSVF and funding requirements Meeting eligibility criteria does not guarantee financial assistance. Hours: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION CURRY SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers housing-focused case management, referrals to community services, limited short-term rental or deposit assistance, and housing placement support. All assistance is time-limited and based on program guidelines. 937 Chetco Avenue Suite D Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Veteran households may be considered for SSVF if they: -Include at least one eligible veteran (per VA guidelines) -Are homeless or at risk of homelessness -Have a household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) for Coos or Curry County -Meet additional SSVF and funding requirements Meeting eligibility criteria does not guarantee financial assistance. Hours: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS VETERAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in costs and rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 7916 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans and their households, who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income. Households are only eligible if the veteran applies and is eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Walk in: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (855) 425-5544 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK GARDEN VALLEY SERVICE CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Supportive Services for Veteran Families (SSVF) short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. May be able to assist with rent, deposits, utilities, job search costs, child care costs, or clothing or tools needed for work. 251 NE Garden Valley Boulevard Suite 111 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: -Veterans households at or below 60% State Median Income -Lack housing or are at the risk of losing it -Household only needs short term help to get into stable housing Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (800) 301-8226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK JOSEPHINE COUNTY SERVICE CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Supportive Services for Veteran Families (SSVF) short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless.
